name: Environment Variable Management Across Environments
description: Configure and validate environment variables for development, staging, and production in React and Next.js projects.

steps.title: Create environment files for each environment
steps.description: Set up .env files for local, development, and production environments.
steps.warning: Never commit .env.local or files containing secrets. Add them to .gitignore.

steps.title: Create a .env.example file
steps.description: Document all required environment variables without actual values.
steps.tip: Commit .env.example to the repository so other developers know which variables are needed.

steps.title: Add runtime validation with zod
steps.description: Validate environment variables at startup to fail fast on missing configuration.

steps.title: Add env files to .gitignore
steps.description: Ensure sensitive environment files are not committed to version control.

steps.title: Verify environment loading in development
steps.description: Check that Next.js loads the correct variables at runtime.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Environment Variable Setup
set -euo pipefail

echo "=== Creating environment files ==="

cat > .env.example << 'EOF'
# API Configuration
NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=
NEXT_PUBLIC_SITE_URL=

# Database
DATABASE_URL=

# Authentication
JWT_SECRET=
NEXTAUTH_SECRET=
NEXTAUTH_URL=
EOF

cp .env.example .env.local
echo "NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL=http://localhost:3001/api" > .env.local

echo "=== Updating .gitignore ==="
grep -qxF '.env.local' .gitignore 2>/dev/null || echo -e '\n.env\n.env.local\n.env.*.local' >> .gitignore

echo ""
echo "=== Environment files created ==="
echo ".env.example  - Committed template (no secrets)"
echo ".env.local    - Local development (gitignored)"
echo ""
echo "For validation, install zod: pnpm add zod"

faq.question: What is the difference between NEXT_PUBLIC_ and server-only variables?
faq.answer: Variables prefixed with NEXT_PUBLIC_ are bundled into the client-side JavaScript and visible to users. Server-only variables (without the prefix) are only available in server-side code like API routes and server components. Never put secrets in NEXT_PUBLIC_ variables.

faq.question: How does Next.js load .env files?
faq.answer: Next.js loads .env, .env.local, .env.[environment], and .env.[environment].local in that order. Later files override earlier ones. .env.local always overrides and is meant for machine-specific secrets.

faq.question: Should I use a secrets manager instead of .env files?
faq.answer: For production, yes. Use your platform's secrets management (Vercel Environment Variables, AWS Secrets Manager, Vault) rather than .env files. .env files are best for local development only.
